content:
-----

HOW THIS SHAPES DOWNSTREAM RECRUITMENT

When I prospect the next writer/dramaturgist—whether it’s a composer, choreographer, visual artist, or playwright—I can say:

> “Chris Notarnicola spent four months developing the narrative architecture for this system. Here’s what we learned about how dramatic intent survives audience agency. Here’s the parameter-mapping methodology we evolved together. Here’s the working prototype and the documented case study. Here’s the handbook we wrote for exactly this moment. Now: where do your dramaturgical questions live? How do you want to build on this foundation? What does narrative agency mean in your discipline?”

That’s infinitely more credible and generative than: “We have a system; write something for it.”

You’re not just creating a piece. You’re creating the language that all future collaborations inherit from. You’re establishing the intellectual foundation. Every writer who works with me after you will be standing on the ground you helped establish.

-----
